data.narrative:
LuxWall is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $84M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five private peers at the same stage—Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M)—spanning a valuation range of $120M to $500M. One public comparable (LNZA) is noted for reference.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression, yields $160M based on the $84M raised as a proxy. The peer-median method, anchored to the five same-sector, same-stage comparables, produces a $310M implied valuation, reflecting the median across the peer set. These methods imply a valuation range of $136M to $357M, with a median of $235M.

The analysis supports a valuation of $235M (median), bounded by $136M (low) and $357M (high). Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x stage multiplier for LuxWall's specific operational profile, the degree to which peer companies are truly comparable on technology maturity and market traction, and whether the peer median at $310M adequately reflects sector-wide investor sentiment shifts since the comparables' most recent funding events.
